continue: Big polarized improvement — easy miles jumped to 43% (from 33%) and the gray zone fell to 38%. Every Germany run was genuinely easy (today: 6 mi @ avg HR 127), and that's precisely what's pulling you toward the 80/20 a sub-3 build needs.

You stayed consistent through travel — ~19 mi this week (home run Mon, a travel-day treadmill shakeout Tue, easy runs Thu + today) all easy, despite jet lag. That discipline is exactly what a long build is made of.
Resting metrics are holding for a trip — HRV factor still strong and today's 6-miler was easy and controlled. You're managing the travel well.
change: Polarized split is now 43% easy / 38% gray / 19% hard — a real step toward ~80% easy / ~20% hard. The remaining gray is mostly older moderate runs aging out of the 30-day window; keep new easy days truly easy and it'll keep climbing.

You're in a planned deload — ACWR dropped to 0.6 (“Low”) and Garmin reads “Recovery” because the travel week cut your training load. That's by design, not a problem; you'll be fresh to rebuild. Ease back into volume next week when you're home.

Jet lag is showing in sleep — last night 70 (fair, 7.7 h) and your sleep-history factor is “poor” from the trip. Prioritize sleep + morning daylight the next few days; it's your highest-leverage recovery lever right now.
Aerobic base remains the #1 job (Garmin still flags “Aerobic Low Shortage”). Back home and rested, resume the consistent easy-volume ramp toward 40–50 mi/wk.
Weight is paused at 173.7 (Jun 22) — no scale on the road. Re-log a morning weigh-in when you're home to keep the trend tracking toward your low-160s.
